result:
I’d dissolve the illusion of separation — the idea that we are fundamentally divided from each other, from nature, or even from our own sense of wholeness.

That belief in separation seems to be the root of so much: greed over shared resources, fear of the “other,” exploitation of nature as if it’s external to us, and the lonely struggle within ourselves.  
If people deeply felt — not just intellectually understood — that we’re interwoven, interdependent, part of the same living system, I think many harmful structures and habits would simply cease to make sense.

Empathy would become reflexive. Sustainability would be self-care. Justice would be healing a part of ourselves. And perhaps we could turn our immense creativity toward flourishing, rather than fragmentation.
